sections.paragraph:
Ὑμέναιος, ου, ὁ (esp. in mythol., also GDI 251, 5) Hymenaeus, handed over (w. Alexander) to Satan because of defection fr. the true faith 1 Ti 1:20. Acc. to 2 Ti 2:17 his error and that of Philetus consisted in maintaining that the resurrection had already taken place (cp. AcPlTh 14 [Aa I 245, 5]; of the magician Menander: Just., A I, 26, 4 and Iren. 1, 23, 5 [Harv. I 195]).—DELG s.v. 2 ὑμήν. LGPN I. M-M.

esp. esp. = especially

GDI GDI = Sammlung der griechischen Dialekt-Inschriften—List 3

  1. fr. = from
  2. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

AcPlTh AcPlTh = Acts of Paul and Thecla—List 5

Aa Aa = Acta apostolorum apocrypha—List 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Iren. Iren. = Irenaeus, Haereses, II a.d.—List 5

Harv. Harv. = WHarvey; s. Iren.—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

LGPN LGPN = A Lexicon of Greek Personal Names—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1027
